decisions.decision: Implement the success-path breadth check as one shared `subTest(...)` matrix inside `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests`, using the existing request helpers to exercise all five protected POST routes while keeping ProbabilityEdit as the canonical proof for repeated quota behavior, `429`, and per-agent isolation.
alternatives_rejected: Add five standalone route-specific header tests for the breadth sweep.
Clone the full multi-request limiter matrix onto every protected POST route in this step.

decisions.decision: Assert the full shared `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)` contract on the new non-ProbabilityEdit success checks, with exact `limit` and `remaining` values on a single successful request per route instead of weakening the new coverage to header presence only.
alternatives_rejected: Only assert that some `X-RateLimit-*` headers are present.
Skip exact quota assertions for non-ProbabilityEdit routes to avoid repetition.

decisions.decision: Preserve the current disabled-limiter and invalid-agent resolve seams, and add a separate limiter-enabled resolve success assertion rather than rewriting the existing resolve auth test into an enabled-limiter-only variant.
alternatives_rejected: Replace the current resolve auth test wholesale and lose the disabled-limiter no-header proof.
Keep the stale resolve success expectation that headers stay absent when the limiter is enabled.

decisions.decision: Give comment-post first-class coverage with a dedicated success-path HTTP test that asserts both the accepted comment payload and the shared rate-limit headers, while create-market and event-trade participate as explicit named cases in the shared breadth matrix.
alternatives_rejected: Cover comment-post only as an anonymous loop case.
Add standalone route-specific success-path header tests for create-market and event-trade even though they already have general HTTP success coverage elsewhere.

steps.task: Audit the current `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` helpers and assertions in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` to identify the stale resolve success expectation, the existing ProbabilityEdit header helper usage, and the uncovered success-path seams for comment-post, create-market, and event-trade.

Add a shared success-path breadth test in `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` that runs with a positive `RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN` and explicit `subTest(...)` labels for create-market, probability-edit, market-resolve, comment-post, and event-trade, asserting `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)` after one accepted request for each route.

Add a dedicated comment-post success test in the same class that asserts the accepted `comment` response contract together with the rate-limit headers so comment-post ends the step with route-specific, first-class auth/rate-limit coverage.

Refactor the resolve coverage just enough to keep missing-agent, blank-agent, malformed-agent, and disabled-limiter no-header assertions intact while moving the limiter-enabled resolve success assertion to header presence via the shared helper.

Run the focused `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` slice that covers the new success-path header assertions, inspect the diff to confirm the change stays in test coverage, and commit the worktree with the requested message.
